notes.content: DATED MATERIAL, SUBJECT TO CHANGE. PLEASE CONSULT CURRENT UCSD GENERAL CATALOG FOR ANY ADDITIONAL INFORMATION. Effective Fall 2023, major preparation will be required for this major. For details, visit: http://admissions.ucsd.edu/MajorPrep Transfer students must complete the following preparatory courses for the Real Estate & Development Major prior to enrolling at UC San Diego. ECON/MGT 4 ECON 1 MATH 10A or 20A RECOMMENDED: MGT 5 We recommend students also take MGT 5 as this class is a requirement for UC San Diego Real Estate and Development majors The curriculum for the Bachelor of Science in Real Estate and Development is broad and interdisciplinary in response to the diverse ways in which real estate impacts the ways people live, work, and enrich their lives. Required courses include economics, business management, urban planning, real estate finance, real estate law, sustainable development, and urban design. All coursework is designed to facilitate qualitative, quantitative, analytical, strategic, design, and problem-solving, solutions-oriented skills. Majors are encouraged to pair their degree with a minor degree in one of the many minor degrees offered at UC San Diego, particularly those offered by the Rady School of Management including the minor in business and the minor in entrepreneurship and innovation. For more information please visit usp.ucsd.edu .
notes.contentHtml:
DATED MATERIAL, SUBJECT TO CHANGE. PLEASE CONSULT CURRENT UCSD GENERAL CATALOG FOR ANY ADDITIONAL INFORMATION.


Effective Fall 2023, major preparation will be required for this major. For details, visit: http://admissions.ucsd.edu/MajorPrep


Transfer students must complete the following preparatory courses for the Real Estate & Development Major prior to enrolling at UC San Diego.


  • ECON/MGT 4
  • ECON 1
  • MATH 10A or 20A


RECOMMENDED:

MGT 5

We recommend students also take MGT 5 as this class is a requirement for UC San Diego Real Estate and Development majors


The curriculum for the Bachelor of Science in Real Estate and Development is broad and interdisciplinary in response to the diverse ways in which real estate impacts the ways people live, work, and enrich their lives. Required courses include economics, business management, urban planning, real estate finance, real estate law, sustainable development, and urban design. All coursework is designed to facilitate qualitative, quantitative, analytical, strategic, design, and problem-solving, solutions-oriented skills. Majors are encouraged to pair their degree with a minor degree in one of the many minor degrees offered at UC San Diego, particularly those offered by the Rady School of Management including the minor in business and the minor in entrepreneurship and innovation. For more information please visit usp.ucsd.edu.
